title: Simplify Alias Modal UX with Tooltip Explanation
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want the alias modal to have a simplified layout with a clear tooltip explaining what an alias is, so that I can quickly understand and add alternative names for metrics.
description:
The alias management modal was simplified to reduce visual clutter:

1. **Removed** the card class and card header wrapper, leaving just the input text box and action button
2. **Added** an "i" (info) icon to the header with a tooltip explaining the concept: *"Alias is an alternative name that you use for a metric name. Example: SQL, Sales Qualified Lead and Qualified Lead could all mean the same thing."*

This streamlines the UX for adding and managing metric aliases.
acceptance_criteria: Alias modal no longer uses card class or card header styling
An info icon appears in the alias section header
Hovering the info icon displays a tooltip explaining what an alias is
Tooltip text reads: 'Alias is an alternative name that you use for a metric name. Example: SQL, Sales Qualified Lead and Qualified Lead could all mean the same thing.'
Input text box and action button remain functional
ux_changes: Simplified the alias modal by removing the card wrapper and adding an info icon tooltip. The modal now shows just the input field and button with a contextual tooltip explaining the alias concept.
implementation_notes: Initial implementation had a z-index issue where the tooltip rendered behind the popup. This was resolved by consulting a front-end developer for the correct tooltip positioning.
comment_summary: Developer initially had a tooltip z-index issue with the tooltip showing behind the modal popup. After consulting with a front-end colleague, the issue was resolved and the change was completed.
